title: gastrolobium nervosum
intro: Gastrolobium nervosum, also known as the Nervous Flower or Poison, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its toxic alkaloids and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce vomiting, treat snake bites, and reduce fever. Gastrolobium nervosum is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from pain. Herbal preparations of the plant, although often toxic in large doses, have been used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, but caution and expert guidance are highly recommended due to its toxicity.
